scheme_candidates.bbox_note: Upper half overview schemes comparing Claisen and aza-Claisen variants.
scheme_candidates.vision_summary: Overview of Claisen vs aza-Claisen rearrangements, including neutral, charged, and keteneimine variants of 3-aza-1,5-hexadienes.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: The thermal [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement of N-allyl enamines is called the aza-Claisen rearrangement; milder temperatures are required as one progresses from neutral to charged to keteneimine systems.

extracts.transformation_text: Thermal or Lewis-acid-promoted aza-Claisen rearrangement of N-allyl enamines / aza-hexadiene systems.
extracts.notes_text: Page compares ordinary Claisen rearrangement with aza-Claisen variants.

scheme_candidates.bbox_note: Chairlike transition-state scheme in the middle-lower panel.
scheme_candidates.vision_summary: Concerted aza-Claisen rearrangement proceeding through a chairlike transition state with quasi-equatorial substituent arrangement.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: The aza-Claisen rearrangement is a concerted process and usually takes place via a chairlike transition state.

extracts.transformation_text: Concerted aza-Claisen rearrangement through a chairlike transition state.

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: Carboxamide enolate aza-Claisen rearrangement used in the enantioselective total synthesis of (-)-isoiridomyrmecin.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: S. Ito et al. utilized the aza-Claisen rearrangement of carboxamide enolates for the enantioselective total synthesis of (-)-isoiridomyrmecin.

extracts.transformation_text: Carboxamide enolate aza-Claisen rearrangement in the synthesis of (-)-isoiridomyrmecin.
extracts.reactants_text: Chiral carboxamide / allyl substrate shown on page
extracts.notes_text: Product isolated as a single (R,R) stereoisomer according to page text.

result.page_title: BALDWIN'S RULES / GUIDELINES FOR RING-CLOSING REACTIONS

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: Oxime brosylate undergoes Beckmann rearrangement to lactam intermediates, then onward to (+)-codeine.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: J.D. White used a Beckmann rearrangement of a cyclopentanone oxime derivative in the asymmetric total synthesis of non-natural (+)-codeine.

extracts.reagents_text: 1. HONH2·HCl, NaOAc; 2. BsCl, Et3N, DMAP; 3. AcOH
extracts.yield_text: 90% (oxime formation); 69% for 2 steps after brosylation / rearrangement
extracts.notes_text: Two lactam isomers formed in an 11:1 ratio favoring the desired isomer.

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: anti-Oxime converted under sulfonylation / Beckmann conditions to a seven-membered lactam, then elaborated to (-)-ibogamine.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: J.D. White reported synthesis of (-)-ibogamine via a Beckmann rearrangement of an anti oxime in the presence of p-toluenesulfonyl chloride.

extracts.time_text: 3 h reflux for oxime formation; 3 h for sulfonylation / rearrangement
extracts.notes_text: The azatricyclic framework was established through Beckmann rearrangement.

scheme_candidates.bbox_note: Bottom panel: photo-Beckmann in sparteine synthesis.
scheme_candidates.vision_summary: Hydroxylamine precursor converted in situ to nitrone and photolyzed to lactam, then reduced to (+)-sparteine.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: J. Aubé used a photo-Beckmann rearrangement in the endgame of (+)-sparteine synthesis.

extracts.reactants_text: Hydroxylamine / ketone precursor shown on page
extracts.reagents_text: TFA, 4A molecular sieves; NaHCO3; hν (254 nm); LAH
extracts.notes_text: Hydroxylamine is generated in situ and reacts intramolecularly with the ketone before photolysis.

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: Complex tricyclic tertiary alcohol dehydrated with Burgess reagent to an exocyclic alkene in the taxol synthesis.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: R. Holton used the Burgess dehydration reaction to install an exo-methylene group in the first total synthesis of taxol.

extracts.transformation_text: Burgess dehydration of a tricyclic tertiary alcohol to an exocyclic alkene.
extracts.notes_text: Used to set the stage for D-ring (oxetane) formation in taxol synthesis.

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: Burgess cyclodehydration gives an oxazoline, followed by oxidation to an oxazole in the synthesis of (-)-madumycin II.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: A.I. Meyers used Burgess cyclodehydration followed by oxidation in the first enantioselective synthesis of (-)-madumycin II.

extracts.transformation_text: Burgess cyclodehydration / oxidation sequence in the synthesis of (-)-madumycin II.
extracts.reactants_text: Hydroxy amide / masked dehydropeptide precursor shown on page
extracts.notes_text: The oxazole moiety is introduced in two steps via Burgess cyclodehydration then oxidation.

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: Secondary alcohol removed with Burgess reagent to form an α,β-unsaturated ketone, followed by hydrogenation / deprotection steps to herbicidin B.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: A. Matsuda used the Burgess reagent to remove a secondary alcohol functionality in a route to herbicidin B.

extracts.transformation_text: Burgess-reagent-mediated dehydration en route to Herbicidin B.
extracts.reagents_text: Burgess reagent; 1. Pd/C, HCO2NH4; 2. Sm, I2; 3. TBAF
extracts.notes_text: The corresponding α,β-unsaturated ketone was isolated in good yield before conversion to herbicidin B.

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: Ireland-Claisen rearrangement used to set three stereocenters in a substituted alkene precursor, followed by hydrolysis / homologation to ebelactone A.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: I. Paterson used the Ireland-Claisen rearrangement in the synthesis of (-)-ebelactone A and B.

extracts.transformation_text: Ireland-Claisen rearrangement en route to (-)-Ebelactone A / B.
extracts.reactants_text: β-Lactone-containing allyl ester precursor shown on page
extracts.reagents_text: TMSCl, Et3N, LDA; 1 N HCl; CH2N2 / Et2O
extracts.time_text: 1 h for silyl ketene acetal formation
extracts.notes_text: Page text states 96:4 diastereoselectivity, indicating highly (E)-selective silyl ketene acetal formation.

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: Claisen-Ireland rearrangement of a bicyclic allyl ester followed by esterification gives an intermediate used in the convergent synthesis of aspidophytine.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: E.J. Corey used the Claisen-Ireland rearrangement to construct a key intermediate in the first convergent enantioselective total synthesis of aspidophytine.

extracts.transformation_text: Claisen-Ireland rearrangement en route to Aspidophytine.
extracts.reactants_text: Bicyclic allyl ester substrate shown on page
extracts.reagents_text: 1. LDA / TBSCl, THF, -78 °C then heat ([3,3]); 2. EDCI, i-PrOH, DMAP
extracts.notes_text: Used to create a key convergent intermediate in the aspidophytine synthesis.

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: Optically active trichodiene prepared via Claisen-Ireland rearrangement that forges vicinal quaternary centers with complete facial selectivity.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: J.C. Gilbert used a Claisen-Ireland rearrangement as the key step in the first chemical synthesis of optically active (-)-trichodiene.

extracts.reactants_text: Methoxy-substituted allyl ester / cyclopentyl substrate shown on page
extracts.intermediates_text: Predominantly one chairlike transition state shown on page
extracts.reagents_text: 1. LDA, TMSCl, TEA, THF; 2. reflux; 3. H3O+; 4. CH2N2, Et2O
extracts.notes_text: Page states complete facial selectivity and excellent diastereoselectivity.

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: Acyl-substituted 1,3-dicarbonyl heterocycles reduced to corresponding alkyl analogs under Zn / acid Clemmensen conditions.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: T. Kappe reduced 3-acyl-4-hydroxy-2(1H)-quinolones and 3-acyl-4-hydroxy-6-methylpyran-2-ones using zinc powder in acetic acid / hydrochloric acid.

extracts.transformation_text: Reduction of acyl-substituted heterocyclic 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds to alkyl analogs.
extracts.notes_text: Representative examples shown for R = CH2Ph and R = CH3.

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: cis-Fused decahydroquinolone deoxygenated under Clemmensen conditions in the synthesis of pumiliotoxin C.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: C. Kibayashi used the Clemmensen reduction in the endgame of (-)-pumiliotoxin C synthesis.

extracts.transformation_text: Clemmensen reduction en route to 5-Epi-Pumiliotoxin C.
extracts.notes_text: Subsequent debenzylation converted the major isomer into 5-epi-pumiliotoxin C.

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: Tricyclic piperidone exposed to Clemmensen conditions gives mainly elimination product and minor desired alkane, relevant to structure revision of lepadiformine.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: S.M. Weinreb used Clemmensen conditions to remove a ketone in a lepadiformine synthesis, leading mainly to elimination and prompting structure revision.

extracts.transformation_text: Clemmensen reduction / elimination sequence in the revision of lepadiformine.
extracts.products_text: Elimination product; desired reduced alkane; putative structure of Lepadiformine
extracts.yield_text: 51% elimination product; 6% reduced alkane; 70% hydrogenation of elimination product
extracts.notes_text: Under these conditions the minor elimination product was formed predominantly.

scheme_candidates.vision_summary: Ketone functionality removed under Clemmensen conditions in a route to novel tetracyclic undecane derivatives, followed by hydrogenation and oxidation.
scheme_candidates.nearby_text: F.J.C. Martins used the Clemmensen reduction in a synthesis of novel tetracyclic undecane derivatives.

extracts.transformation_text: Clemmensen reduction in a route to a novel tetracyclic undecane derivative.
extracts.reactants_text: Tetracyclic ketone / alcohol precursor shown on page
extracts.products_text: Reduced intermediate; novel tetracyclic undecane derivative
extracts.reagents_text: 6 N HCl / DCM, Zn(Hg); H2 / Pt; AcOH, CrO3
extracts.notes_text: Three-step sequence shown after the Clemmensen deoxygenation.
